entry.title: Writing Plan
entry.content: I will list the contents I want to discuss here Perfectoid and Direct Summand Conjecture Hex(a Math Club talk) Maybe more

entry.title: zelda-side-quests-stables
entry.content: Stables #A ROYAL RECIPE 在Riverside Stable（Hyrule Filed东南边）和Gotter对话。他要你取回在Hyrule Castle的一些东西。在Hyrule Castle的图书馆，你可以找到两本食谱：一本第一层，木头做的大桌子上。第二本在第二次走道的书架上。 第一本食谱（水果蛋糕）：2个或3个不同的水果，加上tabantha wheat（小麦）和cane sugar（甘蔗）。 第二本食谱（怪物蛋糕）：怪物的提取物（从Kilton那掉落，或者来自在Hyrule Field被怪物攻击的旅行者的礼物），加上goat butter（山羊奶油）tabantha wheat（小麦）和cane sugar（甘蔗）。 把上面两个食物给 Gotter，他会给你银色卢比。之后他愿意继续用银色卢比换你的蛋糕。（goat butter，cane sugar，Tabantha wheat在Rito Village有得买 #MISKO, THE GREAT BANDIT 在Dueling Peaks Stable和Domidak，Prissen对话。如果你愿意向他们支付100卢比，他们会向你提供关于一个藏有秘密的宝藏洞穴的谜语。 这个任务的秘密宝藏洞穴在Dueling Peaks的东南方，在主河流的分支的小溪流的尽头，爬到悬崖上看到个可破坏的石头，用炸弹炸碎它们，你会看到一大堆的宝藏（洞穴里还有个可破坏的石头），开了宝箱就会完成这个任务。早点做这个任务你会得到十分有用的武器，在与敌人对抗中提供了巨大的帮助。

entry.title: [Zelda]支线任务
entry.content: Zelda 支线任务 DUELING PEAKS TOWER地区： Misko, the Great Bandit [Dueling Peaks Stable] The Priceless Maracas [West Necluda] WILD HORSES [Dueling Peaks Stable] Flown The Coop [Kakariko Village] ARROWS OF BURNING HEAT Koko’s Kitchen [Kakariko Village] Cooking with Koko [Kakariko Village] Koko Cuisine [Kakariko Village] Koko’s Specialty [Kakariko Village] PLAYTIME WITH COTTLA FIND THE FAIPY FOUNTAIN HATENO TOWER地区： The Statue’s Bargain [Hateno Village] THE WEAPON CONNOISSEuR A GIFT FOR MY BELOVE THE SHEEP RUSTLERS Robbie’s Research [Hateno Acient Tech Lab] Slated for Upgrades [Hateno Acient Tech Lab] SUNSHROOM SENSING THE HERO’S CACHE Hylian Homeowner [Hateno Village] From the Ground Up [Hateno Village] LANAYRU TOWER地区： Special Delivery [Bank of Wishes] Diving is Beauty [Zora’s Domain] Lynel Safari [Zora’s Domain] Frog Catching [Zora’s Domain] The Giant of Ralis Pond [Zora’s Domain] Luminous Stone Gathering [Zora’s Domain] A Wife Washed Away [Zora’s Domain] RIVERBED REWARD Zora Stone Monuments [Zora’s Domain] By Firefly’s Light [Kakariko Village] FARON TOWER地区： WHAT’S FOR DINNER? Sunken TREASURE TAKE BACK THE SEA A GIFT OF NIGHTSHADED Thunder Magnet [Lakeside Stable] LAKE TOWER 地区： HUNT FOR THE GIANT HORSE THE HORSEBACK HOODLUMS WASTELAND TOWER地区： GOOD SIZED HORSE MISSING IN ACTION RUSHROOM RUSH AN ICE CUY Tools of the Trade [Gerudo Town] The Secret Club’s Secret [Gerudo Town] Medicinal Molduga [Gerudo Town] THE EIGHTH HEROINE THE FORGOTHEN SWOED The Thunder Helm [Gerudo Town] The Mystery Polluter [Gerudo Town] The Search For Barta [Gerudo Town] TABANTHA TOWER地区： CURRY FOR WHAT AILS YOU THE APPLE OF MY EYE THE SPARK OF ROMANCE FACE THE FROST TALUS Find Kheel [Rito Village] HEBRA TOWER地区： Stalhorse: Pictured！ WOODLAND TOWER 地区： Ballon Flight [Woodland Stable] A Freezing Rod [Great Hyrule Forest] THE KOROK TRIALS LEGENDARY RABBIT TRAIL Leviathan Bones [Serenne Stable] A GIFT FROM THE MONKS RIDDLES OF HYRULE CENTRAL & RIDGELAND TOWER地区： A Gift for the Great Fairy [Tabantha Bridge Stable] THE ROYAL GUARD’S GEAR A Rare Find [Outskirt Stable] My Hero [Outskirt Stable] A Royal Recipe [Riverside Stable] The Royal White Stallion [Outskirt Stable] ELDIN TONWER 地区： Fireproof Lizard Roundup [Southern Mine] The Road to Respect [Goron City] DEATHMOUNTAIN’S SECRET The Jewel Trade [Goron City] AKKALA TOWER 地区： A PARENT’S LOVE HOBBIES OF THE RICH A SHADY CUSTOMER LITTLE SISTER’S BIG REQUEST

entry.title: Etale Cohomology(2)
entry.content: This is a note of a summer mini-course in University of Michigan taught by David Schwein. Bosch Nero Model$X_\mathrm{et}$ for topolocial space $X$ is different from $X_\mathrm{et}$ for scheme $X$. $$F\mapsto \lim_{L\supseteq k \text{ finite Gal}} F(\mathrm{spec}(L))$$is functorial TheoremAbove map is an equivalence between $\mathrm{Sh}(\mathrm{spec}(k)_{\mathrm{et}})$ and the category of discrete $G_k$-Sets. $H^i(G_k,M)$: Galois cohomology $H^i(\mathrm{spec}(k)_\mathrm{et},F)$: etale cohomology $H^i(G_k,M)\rightarrow^{\cong}H^i(\mathrm{spec}(k)_\mathrm{et},F)$ Sheafification PropositionThere is a functor $\mathrm{PSh}(\mathcal C)\rightarrow \mathrm{Sh}(\mathcal C)$ left adjoint to $\mathrm{Sh}(\mathcal C)\rightarrow \mathrm{PSh}(\mathcal C)$ Theorem$\mathrm{Ab}(\mathcal C)$ is abelian with enough injectives Etale ToposEtale MorphismsEtale maps are analogues to local isomorphisms of $\mathbb C$-analytic manifolds. DefinitionLet $X$ and $Y$ be two schemes, the map $\pi:X\rightarrow Y$ is etale if one of the following equivalent conditions holds:1) For any $x\in X$, there is an affine open $x\in U\subseteq X$ and $\pi(x)\in V\subseteq Y$ such that $U\rightarrow V$ is $\mathrm{spec}(A\rightarrow B)$ where $B=A[x_1,…,x_n]/(f_1,…,f_n)$ and $\det(\frac{\partial f_i}{\partial x_j})$ is invertible in $B$.2) For any $x\in X$, there is an affine open $x\in U\subseteq X$ and $\pi(x)\in V\subseteq Y$ such that $U\rightarrow V$ is $\mathrm{spec}(A\rightarrow B)$ where $B=A[x]_g/(f)$ and $f’$ is invertible in $B$.3) $\pi$ is locally of finite presentation and flat, and $\forall y\in Y$, $\pi^{-1}(y)=\coprod_{i=1}^n \mathrm{spec}(L_i)$ where $L_i$’s are finite seperable extension of $k$.4) $\pi$ is flat and unramified5) $\pi$ is lofp and formally etale: for any closed embedding $Z_0\rightarrow Z$ with square zero ideal, there is a unique map $Z_0\rightarrow X$ completing the following diagram$\require{AMScd}$\begin{CD} Z_0 @>>> X \\ @VVV @VVV^\pi \\ Z @>>> Y\end{CD} Examples Open embeddings $\mathrm{spec}(L)\rightarrow \mathrm{spec}(k)$ where $k\subseteq L$ is a finite seperable extension. $\mathrm{spec}(k(x))\rightarrow \mathrm{spec}(k(x^p))$ where $\mathrm{char}(k)=p$. DefinitionLet $X$ be a scheme, the etale site $X_\mathrm{et}$ is the category of etale $X$-schemes (subcategory of $(\mathrm{Scheme},\downarrow,X)$) with topology $(U_i\rightarrow^{\phi_i} U)$ is a cover if $\cup_i \phi_i(U_i)=U$ where each $\phi_i$ is etale. The etale topos is $\mathrm{Sh}(X_\mathrm{et})$.

entry.title: Etale Cohomology(1)
entry.content: This is a note of a summer mini-course in University of Michigan taught by David Schwein. Sites DefinitionA presheaf on a category $\mathcal C$ is a contravariant functor $\mathcal C^\mathrm{op}\rightarrow \mathrm{Set}$ Let $\hat{\mathcal C}$ be the category of presheaves on $\mathcal C$. Then we have following facts: $\hat{\mathcal C}$ is complete: it has all Limits and Colimits (Yoneda Embedding) For each $X\in \mathcal C$, let $h_X$ be the functor such that $h_X(Y)=\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathcal C}(Y,X)$, then $h$ induces an embedding $\mathcal C\rightarrow \hat{\mathcal C}$ Next we want to find out what is a sheaf on a category. For this purpose, we need to understand what is an open cover on a category $\mathcal C$. Therefore we introduce Grothendieck Topology. DefinitionLet $\mathcal C$ be a category with pullbacks. A Grothendieck Topology on $\mathcal C$ is an assignment$$U\mapsto \mathrm{Cov}(U)\subseteq \mathrm{Ob}(\mathcal C,\downarrow,U)$$where $\mathrm{Cov}(U)$ is a collection of objects over $U$ such that1) The isomorphism $U\rightarrow U$ is a cover.2) If $(U_i\rightarrow U|i\in I)$ is a cover of $U$ and $V\rightarrow U$, then $(V\times_U U_i\rightarrow V|i\in I)$ is a cover of $V$.3) If $(U_i\rightarrow U|i\in I)$ is a cover of $U$ and $(U_{ij}\rightarrow U_i|j\in I_j)$ is a cover of $U_i$, then $(U_{ij}\rightarrow U|i,j)$ is a cover of $U$.Exercise If $(U_i\rightarrow X|i\in I)$ and $(V_j\rightarrow X|j\in J)$ are two covers of $X$, then $(U_i\times_X V_j \rightarrow X|i\in I,j\in J)$ is a cover of $X$.AnswerSince $(U_i\rightarrow X|i\in I)$ is a cover of $X$ and we have the identity map $X\rightarrow X$, by Axiom 2 we see that $(U_i\times_X X\rightarrow X|i\in I)$ is a cover of $X$. Now for each $U_i\times_X X$, $(U_i\times_X V_j\rightarrow U_i\times_X X|j\in J)$ is a cover(this is again by axiom 2), so the conclusion follows. DefinitionA site is a category together with a Grothendieck Topology Examples $\mathcal C=\mathrm{Open}(X)$ while $\mathrm{Cov}(U)=(U_i\rightarrow U)$ where $\cup_i U_i = U$. $X$ is an affine scheme, $\mathcal C=X_\mathrm{aff}$ the collection of all distinguished open susbets of $X$. The topology is the same as (1). $\mathcal C=G$-sets (sets with a group $G$ action), the open cover is $(X_i\rightarrow^{\phi_i} X)$ if $\cup_i\phi_i(X_i)=X$. $\mathcal C=\mathrm{Top}$, the open cover is $(X_i\rightarrow^{\phi_i} X)$ if $\cup_i\phi_i(X_i)=X$ where $\phi_i$ is a local homeomorphism (etale) $X$ is a topological space, $\mathcal C=X_{\mathrm{et}}$ (etale spaces over $X$) $\mathcal C=(\mathrm{spec}(k))_\mathrm{et}$ where objects are $\prod_\mathrm{finite} \mathrm{spec}(L)\rightarrow \mathrm{spec}(k)$ where $L$ are finite seperable extensions of $k$. (jointly surjective topology) SheavesDefinitionLet $\mathcal C$ be a site, a presheaf $F\in\hat{\mathcal C}$ is a sheaf if for any $U\in \mathcal C$, any cover $(U_i\rightarrow U|i\in I)$ and any element $s_i\in F(U_i)|i\in I$ such that$$F(U_i\times_U U_j\rightarrow U_i)(s_i)=F(U_i\times_U U_j\rightarrow U_j)(s_j)$$then there is a unique $s\in F(U)$ such that $F(U_i\rightarrow U)(s)=s_i$.Or in other words, we have an equalizer$$F(U)\rightarrow F(U_i)\Rightarrow F(U_i\times_U U_j)$$ We shall use following notations: $\mathrm{PSh}(\mathcal C)$: presheaves of Sets $\mathrm{PAb}(\mathcal C)$: presheaves of Abelian Groups $\mathrm{Sh}(\mathcal C)$: Sheaves of Sets $\mathrm{Ab}(\mathcal C)$: Sheaves of Abelian Groups Exercise Let $X$ be an affine scheme, then the map $\mathrm{Open}(X)\rightarrow X_\mathrm{aff}$ induces equivalence $\mathrm{Sh}(\mathrm{Open}(X))\rightarrow \mathrm{Sh}(X_\mathrm{aff})$ Let $X$ be an topological space, then the map $\mathrm{Open}(X)\rightarrow X_\mathrm{et}$ induces equivalence $\mathrm{Sh}(\mathrm{Open}(X))\rightarrow \mathrm{Sh}(X_\mathrm{et})$ Definition$\mathcal C$ is a Grothendieck topos if $\mathcal C$ is equivalent to $\mathrm{Sh}(\mathcal D)$ for some site $\mathcal D$. Now we look at $\mathrm{spec}(k)_\mathrm{et}$ $L\supseteq k$ is a finite Galois extension, then $G_{L/k}=\mathrm{Aut}_{\mathrm{spec}(k)}(\mathrm{spec}(L))$ For any $F\in\mathrm{Sh}(\mathrm{spec}(k))_\mathrm{et})$, $F(\mathrm{spec}(L))$ is a $G_{L/k}$-set Exercise $L\otimes_k L\rightarrow \prod_{\sigma\in \mathrm{Gal}(L/k)} L$ which sending $x\otimes y$ to $(x\sigma y)_\sigma$ is an isomorphism. We have an equalizer:$$F(\mathrm{spec}(k))\rightarrow F(\mathrm{spec}(L))\Rightarrow F(\mathrm{spec}(L\otimes_k L))$$ Conclusion: $F(\mathrm{spec}(k))=F(\mathrm{spec}(k))^{\mathrm{G}_{L/k}}$ Fact Sheaf cohomology on $\mathrm{spec}(k)_\mathrm{et}$ is Galois cohomology.

entry.title: Properties of Morphisms
entry.content: Here we lost some properties of morphisms Definition of MorphismA morphism of schemes are morphisms of locally ringed spaces Properties of MorphimsOpen EmbeddingA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called open embedding if the it’s an ismoprhism from $X$ to an open subset $U$ in $Y$. QuasicompactA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called quasicompact if for any affine open subset $U\subset Y$, the preimage of $U$ is quasicompact. PropositionQuasicompactness is afﬁne-local on the target. QuasiseperatedA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called quasiseperated if for any affine open subset $U\subset Y$, the preimage of $U$ is a quasiseperated scheme. PropositionQuasiseperatedness is afﬁne-local on the target. Affine MorphismsA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called affine if for any affine open subset $U\subset Y$, the preimage of $U$ is affine. PropositionThe affineness of a morphism is afﬁne-local on the target. FinitenessA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called finite if it is affine and for any affine open subset $U=\operatorname{Spec}(B)$, the preimage of $U$ is $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$ and $A$ is a module-finite over $B$. PropositionFinite morphisms are closed and of finite fiber. IntegralA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called integral if it is affine and for any affine open subset $U=\operatorname{Spec}(B)$, the preimage of $U$ is $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$ and $A$ is a integral over $B$. PropositionIntegral morphisms are closed. Locally of Finite TypeA morphism $X\rightarrow Y$ is called locally of fintie type if for any affine open subset $U=\operatorname{Spec}(B)$ and any $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$ contained in the preimage of $U$, $A$ is a finitely generated $B$-algebra. A morphism locally of finite type is called of finite type if in addition it is quasicompact. PropositionFinite = Integral + of Finite Type

entry.title: Sheaves and Schemes: Properties of Schemes
entry.content:
This is a small note for different properties of schemes. Definition of SchemeFirst of all, quite obviously, we give the definition of isomorphisms of ringed spaces: DefinitionAn isomorphism of ringed spaces $(X,\mathcal O_X)$ and $(Y,\mathcal O_Y)$ consists of following data:1. a homeomorphism $\pi:Xightarrow Y$2. an isomorphism of sheaves $\mathcal O_Yightarrow \pi_*\mathcal O_X$ Now we can give the definition of schemes DefinitionA scheme $(X,\mathcal O_X)$ is a ringed space such that for any point $p\in X$ there is an open neighbourhood $U$ such that $(U,\mathcal O_X|_U)$ is isomorphic to an affine scheme $(A,\operatorname{spec}(A))$ for some ring $A$ A direct observation is that PropositionIf $U$ is an open subset of $X$, then $(U,\mathcal O_X|_U)$ is a scheme. It’s called an open subscheme of $X$. Affine-local PropertiesSome properties are defined for any affine open subset, we want to know whether it is enough to check that certain property over a particular affine open cover. First we have a proposition PropositionSuppose $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$ and $\operatorname{Spec}(B)$ are affine open subschemes of a scheme $X$. Then $\operatorname{Spec}(A)\cap\operatorname{Spec}(B)$ is the union of open subsets that are simultaneously distinguished open subschemes of $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$ and $\operatorname{Spec}(B)$.Then if a property $P$ could be checked over a distinguished open cover, we could therefore pass to a different affine cover using this lemma. Such property $P$ is called Affine-local Property. We summaries this in the following lemma:Affine Communication LemmaLet $P$ be a property enjoyed by some affine open subsets of a scheme $X$, such that1. If an affine open subset $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$ has property $P$, then for any $f\in A$, $\operatorname{Spec}(A_f)$ does too2. If $A$ is covered by $A_{f_i}$ for $1\leq i\leq n$ and all $\operatorname{Spec}(A_{f_i})$ has property $P$, then so does $\operatorname{Spec}(A)$.If one affine cover of $X$ has property $P$, then all affine cover has property $P$. This is quite obvious since once $P$ is ture for some affine cover $\{A_i\}$, for any other affine cover $\{B_j\}$, pick $B_j$, apply the proposition above to any $A_i$ where $A_i\cap B_j
eq\emptyset$. Then we obatin an affine cover for $B_j$ where all subsets have property $P$. Since $B_j$ is quaiscompact, we could choose a finite subcover then by lemma, we know that $B_j$ also has property $P$. PropertiesTopological PropertiesQuasicompactnessThe word “quasicompact” simply means “compact” without Hausdorff. Combine this with the definition of scheme, we have following propostion PropositionA scheme $X$ is quasicompact iff it is a finite union of affine open subschemes.But we have a more interesting proposition below PropositionIf $X$ is a quasicompact scheme, then every point has a closed point in its closure. ProofAssume that $X$ has been covered by affine open subschemes $U_1,…,U_n$, let $p$ be a point in $X$.WLOG assume that $p\in U_1$, if $p$ is closed in $X$ then we are done. If not, pick a closed point (closed in $U_1$) $p_1\in  ar{p}\cap U_1$. If it’s not closed, its closure $ ar{p}_1$ has a nonempty intersection with $U_2$, pick another closed point (closed in $U_2$) $p_2\in ar{p}_1\cap U_2$. Since $X$ is $T_0$, and every open neighbourhood of $ ar{p}_1$ contains $p_2$, there must be some open neighbourhood of $p_2$ doesn’t contain $p_1$. Therefore $p_1
ot\in ar{p}_2$, so $ ar{p}_2\cap U_1=\emptyset$. Now if $p_2$ is not closed, assume WLOG that its closure has nonempty intersection with $U_3$, repeat this process until we reached a point $p_n$ closed in $U_n$, and $ ar{p}_n\cap U_i=\emptyset$ for $1\leq i < n$. Therefore $p_n$ is closed in $X$. QuasiseperatenessA scheme $X$ is called quasiseperated if the intersection of any two quasicompact open sets is quasicompact. Algebraical PropertiesReducenessA scheme $X$ is called reduced if $\mathcal O_X(U)$ is reduced for any open subset $U\subset X$. Proposition1. $X$ is reduced iff all stalks are reduced2. If $X$ is a quasicompact scheme, it sufficies to check reduceness at closed points ProofIf $X$ is reduced, suppose $\mathcal O_{X,p}$ is not reduced, then there is some open neighbourhood $U$ containing $p$ such that $f\in \mathcal O_X(U)$ is nilpotent: there is a smaller open neighbourhood $V$ such that $f^n|_V=0$. But then $f\in\mathcal O_X(V)$ is a nilpotent element. So we get a contradiction!If all stalks are reduced, since $\mathcal O_X(U)ightarrow \prod_p \mathcal O_{X,p}$ is an injection, $O_X(U)$ is reduced.Now if $X$ is quasicompact, and one point $p$ (not necessarily closed) has a nonreduced stalk, we want to show that there is a closed point $q$ whose stalk is also nonreduced: Choose $q$ to be the closed point in the closure of $p$, let $(f,U)$ be the nilpotent element in $\mathcal O_{X,p}$, then there is some $V\subset U$ such that $f^n|_V=0$. Consider the support of $f$ (as a section), it’s closed and $p$ is contained in it. Therefore $q$ is contained in it and $(V,f)$ is a nilpotent element in $\mathcal O_{X,q}$. Reducessness is an affine-local property. (This is obvious from the stalkwise description) IntegralityA scheme $X$ is integral if it is nonempty and $\mathcal O_X(U)$ is an integral domain for every nonempty open subset $U$ of $X$. PropositionA scheme $X$ is integral iff it is irreducible and reduced. ProofIf $X$ is integral, then by definition it is reduced. We only have to show that it is irreducible. Suppose that it is reducible, then there are two nonempty open subsets $U_1,U_2$ such that $U_1\cap U_2=\emptyset$. Consider $U=U_1\cup U_2$, the two nonzero elements $(1,0)$ and $(0,1)$ have zero product, which is a contradiction!Suppose that $X$ is reduced and irreducible. If $X$ is not integral: For some open subset $U\subset X$, there exist $f,g\in\mathcal O_X(U)$ whose product is zero, then let $C_1$ be the zero locus of $f$ as a function and let $C_2$ be the zero locus of $g$ as a function. THen both are closed and $C_1\cup C_2=U$. Since both are nonzero, we have that $C_1$ and $C_2$ are proper subsets of $U$. This contradicts with irreducibility. NoetherianA scheme $X$ is called locally Noetherian if there is an affine cover $\{\operatorname{Spec}(A)\}$ where each $A$ is Noetherian. $X$ is called Noetherian if $X$ is also quasicompact. PropositionLocally Noetherian schemes are quasiseperated Scheme Over $A$A scheme $X$ with a scheme morphism $Xightarrow \operatorname{Spec}(A)$ is called a scheme over $A$, or an $A$-scheme. If $X$ is an $A$-scheme, then the rings of sections over any open subset are $A$-algebras and the restriction maps are $A$-algebra morphisms. Finite TypeAn $A$-scheme $X$ is called locally of finite type if $X$ could be covered by affine open subsets $\operatorname{Spec}(B_i)$ where all $B_i$ are finitely generated $A$-algebra. $X$ is called Of finite type if $X$ is quasicompact. NormalityA scheme $X$ is normal if all stalks are normal. FactorialityA scheme $X$ is factorial if all stalks are UFD. VarietiesAn affine variety over $k$ is a reduced scheme locally of finite type. Referrences THE RISING SEA - Foundations of Algebraic Geometry – Ravi Vakil Can we always find $q\in ar{\{p\}}$ with $p
ot\in ar{\{q\}}$?

entry.title: [Mac]快捷键总结
entry.content: 这里列举一些常用的Mac快捷键 输入特殊字符 字符 按键 栗子 闭音符 Option e + <字母> é 开音符 Option ` + <字母> è 长音符 Option i + <字母> ê 分音符 Option u + <字母> ï 软音符 Option c ç 控制按键 按键 效果 Option Shift <音量+> 增大1/4格音量 Option Shift <音量-> 减小1/4格音量 Option Shift <亮度+> 提高1/4格亮度 Option Shift <亮度-> 降低1/4格亮度

entry.title: [Kindle] 搭建 KindleEar 教程
entry.content: 这里记录一下搭建 KindleEar 的方式 安装Google 账号安全设置首先我们要把 Google 账号的安全设置调成 允许不够安全的应用，点击这个链接 https://myaccount.google.com/security#connectedapps。改为如下入所示 创建Google Cloud项目点击这个链接，然后找到新建项目，如下图所示 项目名称：随意决定一个你喜欢的名字 项目 ID：可以自己修改，但是请记录下这个 ID 后面会用到 创建 GAE点击这里 https://console.cloud.google.com/home/dashboard 来到你创建的 Google Cloud 项目的 Dashboard，找到 Getting Started Tab，点击 Deploy a Hello World app 来创建 GAE 在弹出的窗口中，选择 Python ，然后创建，接着跳过 Tutorial，就创建完成了。 上传APP打开云端的 Console 输入如下代码 1234rm -f uploader.sh* && \wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kindlefere/KindleEar-Uploader/master/uploader.sh && \chmod +x uploader.sh && \./uploader.sh 然后按照提示输入 Gmail 地址和 APPID 就配置完成了 推送打开 http://\<你的APPID\.appspot.com> 你会发现如下页面 默认的用户名和密码都是 admin 登陆后变成 就可以开始愉快的玩耍啦 参考链接 KindleEar 搭建教程：推送 RSS 订阅到 Kindle 好推动，自己造！免费搭建类似狗耳朵的Kindle RSS推送系统

entry.title: Summary of Smooth, Etale and Unramified Algebras
entry.content: Here is a small summary of smooth, etale and unramified maps. An $R$-algebra $S$ is always assumed to be fintiely presented over $R$. Theorem $S$ is etale over $R$ iff near every prime $Q$, $S_b$ is a standard etale algebra over $R_a$ $S$ is unramified over $R$ iff near every prime $Q$, $S_b$ is a holomorphic image by a finitely generated ideal of a standard etale algebra over $R_a$$S$ is smooth over $R$ iff $S_b$ is etale over a polynomial ring in finitely many variables over $R_a$ Theorem$S$ is etale over $R$ iff $S$ is $R$-flat and $\Omega_{S/R}=0$$S$ is unramified over $R$ iff $\Omega_{S/R}=0$(If $R$ contains rational)$S$ is smooth over $R$ iff $S$ is $R$-flat and $\Omega_{S/R}$ is projective. Fiberwise Characteriszation Theorem$S$ is etale over $R$ iff $S$ is $R$-flat and $\kappa_P\otimes S$ is a product of finite seperable algebraic field extension of $\kappa_P$ for any prime $P$$S$ is smooth over $R$ iff $S$ is $R$-flat and $\kappa_P\otimes S$ is geometrically regular over $\kappa_P$

entry.title: TeX学习笔记(2) - 页边距、页眉页脚等
entry.content: 这里主要记录一下如何设置 页边距 页眉、页脚 首行缩进、段落间距 设置页边距使用 geometry 宏包，比如 1234\documentclass{article}\usepackage{geometry}\geometry{left=2.5cm,right=2.5cm,top=2.5cm,bottom=2.5cm}... 就表示把上下左右的距离都设定为2.5cm。 设置页眉、页脚1234567891011121314\documentclass{article}\usepackage{titlesec} %设置页眉页脚的宏包...\begin{document}...\newpagestyle{main}{ \sethead{<左页眉>}{<中页眉>}{<右页眉>} %设置页眉 \setfoot{<左页脚>}{<中页脚>}{<右页脚>} %设置页脚，可以在页脚添加 \thepage 显示页数 \headrule %添加页眉的下划线 \footrule %添加页脚的下划线}\pagestyle{main} %使用该style...\end{document} 注意第一页是默认是不会使用我们定义的style的 设置行首缩进、段落间距12\setlength{\parindent}{0pt} % 统一设定行首缩进为0\setlength{\parskip}{0.5\baselineskip} % 段落间距 参考链接 latex设置页边距和页眉页脚 【LaTeX入门】06、设置页芯、页边距、页眉、页脚 LaTeX技巧459：LaTeX中常见段落格式的设定-字间距，行间距，段间距，缩进

entry.title: TeX学习笔记(1) - 插入图片
entry.content: 这里主要记录了一下如何 插入图片 插入图片123456789101112\documentclass{article}\include{graphicx} % 插入图片用的宏包...\begin{document}...\begin{figure}[h] \centering \includegraphics[height=<图片高度>in]{<图片名字.png>} \caption{<文章中显示的标题>}\end{figure}...\end{document} 假如要插入的是EPS图片，需要额外添加epstopdf包。 分栏12345678910\documentclass{article}\include{multicol} % 分栏用的宏包...\begin{document}...\begin{multicols}{n} % 分成 n 栏...\end{multicols}...\end{document} 假如你想要一栏到达底部再从第二栏开始，应使用 multicols* 环境

entry.title: [Mac] 终极 Shell - zsh
entry.content: zsh 号称是Shel中最强大的一种，这里我们主要介绍一下如何开始使用zsh，内容包括： 开始使用zsh 安装oh my zsh zsh配置 zsh插件 开始使用ZSHMac 系统本身内置了很多shell，可以用如下命令查看 1cat /etc/shells 比如我的系统显示如下： 12345678910# List of acceptable shells for chpass(1).# Ftpd will not allow users to connect who are not using# one of these shells./bin/bash/bin/csh/bin/ksh/bin/sh/bin/tcsh/bin/zsh 要开始使用zsh，只要运行 1chsh -s /bin/zsh 然后输入用户名密码即可。 安装oh my zsh首先安装 git，然后运行 12git clone git://github.com/robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh.git ~/.oh-my-zshcp ~/.oh-my-zsh/templates/zshrc.zsh-template ~/.zshrc 安装完成后退出当前窗口，重新打开一个终端，就可以看到如下画面： 就表示已经安装完成了。这个主题并不好看，这里推荐一下ys主题，简洁大方信息全面。要安装ys主题，打开~/.zshrc，找到ZSH_THEME字段，把这个字段改成ys即可： 1234# Set name of the theme to load. Optionally, if you set this to "random"# it'll load a random theme each time that oh-my-zsh is loaded.# See https://github.com/robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh/wiki/ThemesZSH_THEME="robbyrussell" 改成 1234# Set name of the theme to load. Optionally, if you set this to "random"# it'll load a random theme each time that oh-my-zsh is loaded.# See https://github.com/robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh/wiki/ThemesZSH_THEME="ys" 重新打开zsh，看到如下终端 即可 ZSH配置还是打开~/.zshrc/，这里可以修改我们的各种需求，比如可以设置别名 123alias ll='ls -al'alias -s md=macdown # 在命令行直接输入后缀为 md 的文件名，会在 MacDown 中打开alias -s tex=emacs # 在命令行直接输入 TeX 文件，会在 Emacs 中打开 这里体现出zsh的强大之处：它可以针对不同的文件后缀名调用不同的程序打开，具体就是利用alias -s命令 ZSH插件oh my zsh 本身提供了很多插件，打开~/.zshrc，找到plugin字段，可以添加如下内容 1plugins=(git autojump osx) git插件当打开 git 控制的文件夹的时候，可以显示当前的git branch。并且针对git的很多命令进行了简化，比如参考 1。 autojump插件首先需要安装 autojump，可以使用 brew 安装 1brew install autojump 安装完成后，按下 j+<目录> 可以直接跳转到相应目录 osx插件tab增强 参考链接 终极 Shell oh my zsh 哪些主题比较好看、有特点？

entry.title: [Hexo]在GitHub上搭建博客(4)
entry.content: 这一次，我们要让我们的博客变得更加丰富多彩，首先就是给博客文章插各种各样漂亮的图片。主要包括 如何添加图片 添加CSS支持 添加图片首先我们要启用资源文件夹，打开站点配置文件 ，找到Writing字段： 123456789101112131415# Writingnew_post_name: :title.md # File name of new postsdefault_layout: posttitlecase: false # Transform title into titlecaseexternal_link: true # Open external links in new tabfilename_case: 0render_drafts: falsepost_asset_folder: falserelative_link: falsefuture: truehighlight: enable: true line_number: true auto_detect: false tab_replace: 其中有一个参数叫做post_asset_folder，将它改为true。这样每次我们用hexo new <"new post">的时候，它就会在_post文件夹下自动创建一个同名文件夹，所有和这篇博文相关的东西（比如图片）都可以放到哪里去，比如 1$ hexo new test 然后_post文件夹下就会出现同名的文件和文件夹 然后我们拷贝一张测试图片（test-image.png）到test文件夹里 接着在test.md中写入 1![](test/test-image.png) 效果如图 使用CSS因为Markdown本身是基于HTML的，所以我们只需要直接使用即可 内联使用比如 我是红底白字 就是通过以下代码直接生成 1<span style="background-color:red;color:white">我是红底白字</span> 外部使用Next主题会自动加载如下文件夹下的所有CSS 1<your_site>/themes/next/source/css/ 我们可以修改其中的main.styl文件，加入以下代码： 123// My Css// --------------------------------------------------@import "_mycss/mycss"; 然后创建mycss文件夹，在里面创建mycss.css。这个CSS文件将会被自动加载。 参考链接 Hexo官方文档 - 资源文件夹 hexo博客图片问题 Hexo博客主题NexT使用自定义的CSS样式

entry.title: [Hexo]在GitHub上搭建博客(3)
entry.content: 我们的博客已经基本成型了，这里主要是一些细节性的工作。包括 添加评论支持 添加Google统计 添加搜索服务 添加数学公式支持 使用Tags和Categories 注意到下文中我们会频繁修改两个配置文件，一个是站点配置文件 ，其路径为 1<your_site>/_config.yml 另一个是主题配置文件，其路径为 1<your_site>/themes/next/_config.yml Next主题为什么上文我要推荐Next主题呢，因为里面已经集成了很多插件我们可以直接使用，比如 添加评论支持一个好的博客怎么可以没有评论区，我们可以用Disqus提供的评论服务，到Disqus官网注册账号，然后取得disqus_shortname。打开主题配置文件，添加如下代码到任意行 1disqus_shortname: <your_disqus_shortname> 添加Google统计一个好的博客怎么可以没有统计，我们可以用Google提供的统计工具Google Analytics，注册账号，然后取得Google追踪ID（Google追踪ID总是以UA开头）。打开主题配置文件，修改google_analytics字段 1google_analytics: UA<%%%%%%%%> 添加搜索服务一个好的博客怎么可以没有搜索服务： 首先安装hexo-generator-searchdb，在站点根目录下运行 1$ npm install hexo-generator-searchdb --save 然后编辑站点配置文件 ，添加以下内容到任意区域 12345search: path: search.xml field: post format: html limit: 10000 最后编辑主题配置文件，修改Local search字段： 123# Local searchlocal_search: enable: true 添加数学公式支持一个好的博客怎么可以不是数学相关的！开启MathJax支持异常简单，修改主题配置文件中的MathJax Support字段即可 12# MathJax Supportmathjax: true Tags & Categories添加Tags假如你只有一个标签，只需要在文章的最顶部的tags标签下加上你要加的tag即可: 12345---title: %%%%%%%date: %%%%%%%tags: <Your_tag>--- 假如你有多于一个标签，你可以用两种方式： 123tags:- tag1- tag2 或者 1tags: [tag1, tag2] 添加Tags页面当你给你的文章加上Tags之后，你会发现博客上的Tags链接并不能用。点击之后会提示无法找到页面，这是因为我们还没有创建Tags页面。 打开终端输入以下代码 1$ hexo new page tags 然后在<your_site>/sources/tags/下找到index.md，打开它，在date下面加入 12type: "tags"comment: false #关闭标签页面的评论区域 效果如图 最后打开主题配置文件，找到menu字段，添加tags标签 12345menu: home: / archives: /archives ... tags: /tags 添加Categories在博文的最上方，tags标签下新起一行，假如categories标签，如下所示 123456---title: %%%%%%%date: %%%%%%%tags:categories: <Your_category>--- 添加Categories页面同样的我们还没有创建Categories页面。 打开终端输入以下代码 1$ hexo new page categories 然后在<your_site>/sources/categories/下找到index.md，打开它，在date下面加入 12type: "categories"comment: false #关闭标签页面的评论区域 效果如图 最后打开主题配置文件，找到menu字段，添加categories标签 12345menu: home: / archives: /archives ... categories: /categories 参考链接 多个 Tags 怎么分割？ Hexo使用攻略：（四）Hexo的分类和标签设置 Next主题

entry.title: [Hexo]在GitHub上搭建博客(2)
entry.content: 上次我们介绍了如何建立博客、撰写文章，这次我们来聊聊如何修改博客的一些基本属性。本期主要包括： 修改博客的信息 修改主题样式 发布到GitHub 博客的设定修改博客的基本属性打开<你的博客目录>目录下的_config.yml，这里我们可以修改各种参数信息： 比如Site字段是设置站点信息的 1234567# Sitetitle: Hexosubtitle:description:author: John Doelanguage:timezone: 其中： title就是博客的名称 author就是博客作者 我们可以改成我们想要的，比如说 1234567# Sitetitle: Test Blogsubtitle:description:author: Zerolanguage:timezone: Remark: 所有参数的冒号后面都要加上一个空格 重新把网站发布，就变成了如下的样子 修改主题这里我要推荐一个非常简洁耐用的主题 Next。安装方式如下： 运行命令 1$ git clone https://github.com/iissnan/hexo-theme-next themes/next 打开_config.yml文件，定位到Themes字段 12## Themes: https://hexo.io/themes/theme: landscape 把其中的landscape改成next即可！ 发布上网Github首先你要注册一个GitHub账号，我们发布的原理是GitHub提供一个叫GitHub Page的网页，它对应的网址是https://<user_name>.github.io。而它对应的仓库是<user_name>.github.io。所以你的用户名非常重要，注册的时候请谨慎选取 比如我的GitHub账号就是zeromath，所以对应的主页就是https://zeromath.github.com. 部署添加部署信息打开_config.yml，找到Deployment字段 1234# Deployment## Docs: https://hexo.io/docs/deployment.htmldeploy: type: 在type中填入git，然后在repo里写入你的仓库地址，最后branch里写上master。最后效果如下： 123456# Deployment## Docs: https://hexo.io/docs/deployment.htmldeploy: type: git repo: git@github.com:<user_name>/<user_name>.github.io.git branch: master 添加SSH KEY为了不用每次push都输入密码，我们采用SSH的方式进行push，这就需要将你的SSH KEY添加到GitHub的信任列表里 打开终端，运行如下命令 1$ ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com" 然后一路Enter即可，不用管其他参数。完成后打开~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub文件，将里面的所有内容拷贝出来 打开https://github.com/settings/keys（你可能需要登陆），点击右上角的New SSH key按钮。把刚才拷贝出来的内容拷贝到Key里，然后Title就写上你的电脑名称即可。 部署上网首先我们要安装一个git部署器，运行如下命令 1$ npm install hexo-deployer-git --save 然后就可以愉快的一键部署了！输入 1hexo deploy 然后你的网站就刷刷刷的传到GitHub上啦！打开https://<user_name>.github.io 就可以看到你的网站了。 Remark: 有时候部署完了还需要等一会儿GitHub的网页才能更新 参考链接 Hexo官方文档 - 部署 GitHub Help - SSH Next 主题

entry.title: [Emacs]Emacs学习笔记
entry.content: 主要列出了一些Emacs常用的命令以备查用 缓冲区 作用 命令 函数名 保存当前缓冲区 C-x C-s save-buffer 保存所有缓冲区 C-x s save-some-buffers 列出所有缓冲区 C-x C-b list-buffers 切换缓冲区 C-x b 杀死缓冲区 C-x k kill-buffer 文件 作用 命令 函数名 打开文件 C-x C-f 只读方式打开 C-x C-r 读入另外一个文件代替当前buffer的文件 C-x C-v 文件编辑命令移动插入点 作用 命令 函数名 缓冲区开始 M-> beginning-of-buffer 缓冲区末尾 M-< end-of-buffer 上一屏 C-v scroll-up 下一屏 M-v scroll-down 段落开头 C-{ beginning-of-paragraph 段落结尾 C-} end-of-paragraph 到指定行 M-g g goto-line 行开头 C-a beginning-of-line 行结尾 C-e end-of-line 句子开头 M-a beginning-of-sentence 句子结尾 M-e end=of-sentence 上一行 C-p previous-line 下一行 C-n next-line 上一个单词 M-b backward-word 下一个单词 M-f forward-word 上一个字符 C-b back-char 下一个字符 C-f forward-char 引用插入、删除、撤销和重复 作用 命令 函数 引用插入 C-q <字符值> quote-insert 删除一个字符 C-d delete-char 删除一个单词 M-d kill-word 撤销 C-_ or C-/ undo 重复 C-u <次数> <命令> universal-argument 标记和复制 作用 命令 函数 删除一行 C-k kill-line 设定标记 C-Space or C-@ set-mark-command 全选 C-x h mark-whole-buffer 选取一页 C-x C-p mark-page 选取一段 M-h mark-paragraph 选取一个单词 M-@ mark-word 选取一页 C-x C-p mark-page 交换标记和光标位置 C-x C-x exchange-point-and-mark 将区域中字母改为大写 C-x C-u upcase-region 删除区块 C-w kill-region 复制区块 M-w kill-ring-save 粘贴 C-y yank 快速复制 作用 命令 函数 将区域中内容加入到一个buffer中 append-to-buffer 将区域中内容加入到一个buffer中，不过加入位置在该buffer的光标前 prepend-to-buffer 将区域中内容加入到一个buffer中，删除该buffer原有内容 copy-to-buffer 在该位置插入指定的buffer中所有内容 insert-buffer 将区域中内容复制到一个文件中 append-to-file 字符修改 作用 命令 函数 光标处单词首字母大写 M-c 光标处单词全部字母大写 M-u 光标处单词全部字母小写 M-l 补全 M-/ 交换光标处和之前的字符 C-t 交换光标处和之前的单词 M-t 搜索 作用 命令 函数 从光标处往后搜索 C-s 从光标处往前搜索 C-r (向下)光标位置的单词作为查找字符串 C-s C-w (向下)光标位置到行尾作为查找字符串 C-r C-y 非递增查找 C-s RET <查找字符串> RET 不受换行、空格、标点影响 C-s RET C-w 正则式向下查找 C-M-s 用向上查找命令就将上面命令的s替换为r 宏模式 作用 命令 函数 开始一个宏的定义 C-x ( 结束一个宏的定义 C-x ) 执行宏 C-x e 给最后一个宏命名 name-last-kbd-macro 在当前文件中插入一个已定义并命名过的宏 insert-kbd-macro 参考链接 Emacs 编辑环境，第 1 部分: 学习 Emacs 的基础知识 这里列出了EMACS 的一些常用命令 学习Emacs系列教程（五）：编辑（2）—— 标记 学习Emacs系列教程（六）：编辑（3）

entry.title: [Mac]在Emacs中安装AucTex
entry.content: 这里总结一下在Emacs中安装AucTex和CDLaTeX插件的方式。 必要文件 安装Emacs：直接搜索Emacs，或者到这里下载。 安装MacTex：到这里下载 安装AucTex：打开Emacs，执行M-x list-packages，然后直接搜索AucTex这个包，按i标记，按x执行，就安装完成了 安装cdlatex：复制cdlatex.el文件内容（这里贡献一个我自己用的）到/Applications/Emacs.app/Contents/Resources/site-lisp/cdlatex.el，然后打开.emacs加入以下代码 12345(add-hook 'LaTeX-mode-hook 'LaTeX-math-mode)(add-to-list 'load-path "/Applications/Emacs.app/Contents/Resources/site-lisp/")(add-hook 'LaTeX-mode-hook 'turn-on-cdlatex)(autoload 'cdlatex-mode "cdlatex" "CDLaTeX Mode" t)(autoload 'turn-on-cdlatex "cdlatex" "CDLaTeX Mode" nil) 简单使用教程基础Emacs操作 作用 命令 打开文件 C-x C-f 保存文件 C-x C-s 退出Emacs C-x C-c TeX命令 作用 命令 编译TeX C-c C-c 插入环境 C-c C-e 改变环境 C-u C-c C-e 关闭环境 C-c ] 注释区域 C-c ; 注释段落 C-c % CDLaTeX插件在输入时按下`（键盘 1 键左边的符号），稍等一会儿，就可以打开数学符号面板 此时按下对应的字母／字符键，就可以直接输入对应的符号 继续按下 `或者Tab键，就可以进入循环翻页 在输入时按下'（键盘上单引号按钮）可以打开数学修饰面板 注意很多命令只能在Math-mode下运行（即在$符号之间运行），比如我输入了一个$A$想要变成$\tilde{A}$，只需要把光标定位到A之后的$符号上，按下'，再按下~，就变成了 Q&AQ1: TeX不能识别 Solution: 我们需要添加下列两个到PATH变量中 12/Library/TeX/texbin/usr/local/bin 但是图形界面的Emacs是不会全部加载bash的PATH的，所以直接在bash中修改PATH变量并没有用处，我们还需要添加如下代码到.emacs 12(setenv “PATH”(concat (getenv “PATH”) “:/Library/TeX/texbin:/usr/local/bin”)) Q2: AucTex 预览出错，错误提示为 1PDF2DSC sentinel: Wrong type argument: arrayp, nil Solution: 在.emacs中加入 1(setq preview-gs-command "/usr/local/bin/gs") 重新启动Emacs 参考链接 Mac配置LaTeX和Emacs的编辑环境 OS X + Emacs + TeX AUCTeX/Preview PDF2DSC sentinel error

entry.title: [Hexo]在GitHub上搭建博客(1)
entry.content: 在此整理了一下Hexo博客建立的方法，以下代码演示是基于Mac平台。Windows平台大同小异。 当然最全面的资料是在Hexo官网。这里只是做一个小小的总结： Here We Go！ 博客初建立需要的插件 安装Git 安装Node.js 安装Hexo： 打开终端，输入下列命令 1$ npm install -g hexo-cli 安装文件创建一个你喜欢的文件夹，比如 ~/Documents/Blog，这就是你博客的大本营了。定位到该文件夹下，在根目录下运行 1$ hexo init 当你看到 就表示命令已完成。你会发现文件夹里多了好多东西，但是博客在哪里呢？ 我们来试运行一下，输入命令 12$ hexo generate$ hexo server 提示 打开浏览器，访问http://localhost:4000，就会见到你的博客了 Remark: 在Terminal下按下Ctrl+C就可以取消本地服务器了 第一篇博客创建新文章刚刚我们已经看到了博客中有一篇叫 Hello World 的文章了。这篇文章具体在哪里呢？打开<你的博客目录>/source/_post/，你会发现虾米有一个”hello-world.md”文件，它就是网站上的文章对应的Markdown文件。 Hexo 的所有文章都是通过Markdown书写的，我们也来写一篇自己的文章好了！ 打开Terminal，输入 1$ hexo new <"你的文章标题"> 比如我们要写一篇测试文章，于是输入 1$ hexo new "My first test blog" 系统会提示 1INFO Created: <你的博客目录>/source/_posts/My-first-test-blog.md 重新打开<你的博客目录>/source/_posts/目录，你会发现里面已经有了一个新的MD文件。这就是我们刚刚创建的新文章。 编写文章用任意编辑器（我一般用MacDown或者Emacs）打开”My-first-test-blog.md”，你会发现里面已经写好了一点东西了： 12345---title: My first test blogdate: 2017-03-21 14:48:40tags:--- 其中： title是你最终的文章标题 date就是文章创建的时间，这个可以不用管 tags则是文章的标签，如何加Tags我们以后再讲 在最下面另起一行，你就可以开始写文章了！ 比如你可以写如下文章： 请忽略最上面几行的奇怪行为 然后保存MD文件即可。 发布文章发布到网站文章写好了，要怎么发布呢？ Terminal定位到<你的博客目录>，然后依次输入以下命令 12$ hexo generate$ hexo server 第一个命令hexo generate是生成新的文件，第二个命令是启动server。再次打开http://localhost:4000，我们发现我们的文章已经被po上“网”了！ 删除文章现在有没有觉得那个Hello World很扎眼，我们肯定想要删除它。重新回到<你的博客目录>/source/_posts/目录，找到hello-world.md文件，直接将其删除即可。 有人就要问了，为什么删除了我的浏览器里还是有呢？ 因为我们还没有更新我们的网站，在Terminal下运行 123$ hexo clean$ hexo generate$ hexo server 再刷新网页，是不是就没有了？ 参考链接 教你免费搭建个人博客，Hexo&Github 手把手教你使用Hexo + Github Pages搭建个人独立博客

entry.title: Remarks on Completion of Rings
entry.content: Here are some small remarks on completion of rings and completed rings. Definition of CompletionGiven a ring $R$, not necessarily Noetherian, we could always define its completion $\hat{R}^I$ with respect to an ideal $I\subset R$. Cauchy SequencesOne way to define this is using Cauchy sequence: A sequence $(r_1,r_2,\cdots,r_n,\cdots)$ is a Cauchy sequence if$$\forall n \ \ \exists N \ \text{ such that } \forall i,j\geq N, r_i-r_j\in I^n$$while a null sequence $(r_1,r_2,\cdots,r_n,\cdots)$ is a sequence converges to $0$, that is,$$\forall n \ \ \exists N \ \text{ such that } \forall i\geq N, r_i\in I^n$$ The completion $\hat{R}^I$ is defined to be the ring of all Cauchy sequences modulo the ideal generated by null sequences. Inverse LimitThe completion $\hat{R}^I$ could also be defined as an inverse limit of the system$$R/I\leftarrow R/I^2\leftarrow \cdots \leftarrow R/I^n\leftarrow$$ Completion & LocalizationMaximal ideals of $\hat{R}^I$We have following theorem TheoremThe kernel of the natural map $\hat{R}^I\rightarrow R/I$ is contained in the Jacobson ideal of $\hat{R}^I$. ProofWe use the inverse limit construction. So each element $r$ in $\hat{R}^I$ is denoted by a sequence where $(r_1,r_2,…,r_i,…)$ such that $r_i\in R/I^i$ maps to $r_j\in R/I^j$ for any $i>j$.If $r\in \hat{R}^I$ is in the kernel, then $r={0,r_2,…,r_i,…}$ where each $r_k$ is an element in $I/I^k$. Let $v=(v_1,v_2,…,v_i,…)$ be a sequence such that $v_{i}=1-r_i+r_i^2-\cdots+(-1)^i r_i^i$. Then the image of $v_n$ in $R/I^{n-1}$ is $v_{n-1}$. So $v\in\hat{R}^I$ and we have $(1+r)v=1$. So the kernel is contained in the Jacobson ideal. $\blacksquare$ A direct corollary from this theorem is that we have 1-1 correspondence between maximal ideals of $\hat{R}^I$ and maximal ideals of $R/I$. In particular, if $R/I$ is quasilocal (local but not necessarily Noetherian), then $\hat{R}^I$ is quasilocal. Completion Commutes with LocalizationIf $I=Q$ is a maximal ideal of $R$, then $\hat{R}^Q$ is a quasilocal ring. If we localize $R$ at $Q$ and then take its completion, we also get a quasilocal ring $\hat{R_Q}^Q$. In fact, these two rings are isomorphic to each other as we have following lemma LemmaIf $Q$ is a maximal ideal of $R$, then $R_Q/Q^nR_Q\simeq R/Q^n$ ProofThe only prime ideals in $R/Q^n$ is $Q$ as $Q^n$ is $Q$-primary. So $R/Q^n$ is a quasilocal ring. Therefore the image $R-Q$ is invertible in $R/Q^n$. So we have the desired inverse for the natural map $R/Q^n\rightarrow R_Q/Q^nR_Q$. $\blacksquare$

entry.title: Sheaves and Schemes: Vanishing of Sections
entry.content:
Here we mainly focus on two different ways for a section to vanish. Original ProblemThe problem comes from an exercise (Chapter 1 Ex 3.3(c)) in Hartshorne Show that if $\varphi(X)$ is dense in $Y$, then the map $\varphi^*_P$ is injective for all $P$. Here $X$ and $Y$ are assumed to be varieties. But this could be generalized to morphisms of locally ringed spaces. If $(\varphi,\varphi^\sharp):(X,\mathscr O_X)ightarrow (Y,\mathscr O_Y)$ is a morphism of locally ringed space, $\varphi(X)$ is dense in $Y$ and $X$ is irreducible, then $\varphi^\sharp_P$ is injective for any $P\in X$ Vanishing of SectionsBefore prove this, let’s discuss a little bit about the definition of ‘’section vanishes’’. Two DefinitionsRecall the usual definition of support of a section in a given sheaf $\mathcal F$: DefinitionThe support of $s\in\mathcal F(X)$ is $	ext{supp}(s)=\{P\in X|s_P
ot=0\}$ where $s_P$ is the image of $s$ in the stalk $\mathcal F_P$ The support of a section is always closed because if $P
ot\in 	ext{supp}(s)$, then there is some open subset $U$ containing $P$ such that $s|_U=0$. Therefore $U\cap	ext{supp}(s)=\emptyset$, which shows that $	ext{supp}(s)$ is closed. However, recall the definition of the support of a function, it’s the preimage of $k^*$, which, in particular, is open. If we are considering the sheaf of functions here, then what’s the ‘’correct’’ definition of support of a section (which is a function) corresponding to the definition of support of a function? If you think about this, the answer seems to be immediate. A function $f$ vanishes at a point $P$ when its function value is zero at that point. Think about the definition of the stalk at that point, the image of $f$ has to lie in the maximal ideal of the local ring $\mathcal F_P$. This enlightens us to give following defintion DefinitionThe support of $s\in\mathcal F(X)$ as a function is $	ext{fupp}(s)=\{P\in X|s_P
ot\in m_{X,P}\}$ where $m_{X,P}$ is the maximal ideal in the stalk $\mathcal F_P$ Topological PropertiesWe already saw that $	ext{supp}(s)$ is closed. It turns out that $	ext{fupp}(s)$ is open. Because if $P\in	ext{fupp}(s)$, then $s_P$ is not in the maximal ideal $m_{X,P}$, so it has an inverse $(t,U)$. That is, $st|_U=1$. So they are inverse to each other in any stalk at the points in $U$. Therefore $p\in U\in 	ext{fupp}(c)$. It’s also clear from the definition that $	ext{fupp}(s)\subset	ext{supp}(s)$. For notational reason, let’s denote the complement of $	ext{supp}(s)$ (resp. $	ext{fupp}(s)$) by $	ext{szero}(s)$ (resp. $	ext{fzero}(s)$). Then we already established $	ext{fupp}(s)$ is open and contained in the closed subset $	ext{supp}(s)$ $	ext{szero}(s)$ is open and contained in the closed subset $	ext{fzero}(s)$ The behaviour of the functional support and zero locus is very similar to the behaviour of functions we know. We have following theorems TheoremIf $(\varphi,\varphi^\sharp):(X,\mathscr O_X)ightarrow (Y,\mathscr O_Y)$ is a morphism of locally ringed spaces, then$$ \varphi^{-1}(	ext{fupp}(s))=	ext{fupp}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$$ It’s immediate from the theorem that $$\varphi^{-1}(	ext{fzero}(s))=	ext{fzero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$$ Meanwhile, the behaviour of section is not that good TheoremIf $(\varphi,\varphi^\sharp):(X,\mathscr O_X)ightarrow (Y,\mathscr O_Y)$ is a morphism of locally ringed spaces, then$$ \varphi^{-1}(	ext{szero}(s))\subset	ext{szero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))\subset \varphi^{-1}(	ext{fupp}(s))=	ext{fupp}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$$ The proof of these two theorems comes from the commutative diagram$equire{AMScd}$ egin{CD} \mathscr O_Y(U) @>>> \mathscr O_X(\varphi^{-1}(U)) @. s @>>> \varphi^\sharp (s) \ @VVV @VVV @VVV @VVV \ \mathscr O_{Y,\varphi(P)} @>>> \mathscr O_{X,P} @. s_{\varphi(P)} @>>> \varphi^\sharp(s)_P\end{CD} If $s_{\varphi(P)}$ is zero (resp. contained in the maximal ideal), then $\varphi^\sharp(s)$ maps to zero (resp. contained in the maximal ideal). So $P\in	ext{szero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$ (resp. $P\in	ext{fzero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$). For th converse direction, if $s_{\varphi(P)}$ is not contained in the maximal ideal), then $\varphi^\sharp(s)$ maps to a unit. So $P\in	ext{fupp}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$ Proof of Original ProblemNow we have enough tools to prove our original problem ProofFor any point $P\in X$, let $(f,U)$ be a nonzero element in the stalk $\mathscr O_{Y,\varphi(P)}$. Replace $Y$ with $U$, then $f
eq 0$ means that $\varphi(P)\in	ext{supp}(f)$. We want to prove that $(\varphi^\sharp(f),\varphi^{-1}(U))$ is not zero in the stalk $\mathscr O_{X,P}$. That is equivalent to $P\in	ext{supp}(\varphi^\sharp(f))$Denote $\varphi^\sharp(f)$ by $g$. Assume that $g
ot\in	ext{supp}(g)$, then since $	ext{supp}(g)$ is closed, there is some open subset $V$ such that $p\in V\subset 	ext{szero}(g)$.On the other hand, $\varphi(X)$ is dense in $Y$, so it’s dense in $U$. Therefore $\varphi(X)\cap 	ext{fupp}(f)
eq \emptyset$. So there is some point $Q$ such that $\varphi(Q)\in	ext{fupp}(g)$. But $	ext{fupp}(g)$ is open, so there is some open subset $V’$ such that $Q\in V’\subset 	ext{fupp}(g)$.Note that $X$ is irreducible, $V\cap V’
eq\emptyset$, but this is a contradiction as $	ext{fupp}(g)\cap	ext{fzero}(g)=\emptyset$ and $	ext{szero}(g)\subset 	ext{fzero}(g)$. $ lacksquare$
